---
title: "Testing, Verification, and Observability"
---

# Testing, Verification, and Observability

Range: `1140-1210`.

## Completion

| Status   | Count |
| -------- | ----- |
| authored | 71    |

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                              | Completion | Summary                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| ----- | --------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1140  | [acceptance test](#rune-1140)     | authored   | `acceptance test` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the acceptance test obligation named before work proceeds.          |
| 1141  | [alert](#rune-1141)               | authored   | A threshold that wakes humans or automation when evidence crosses concern.                                                                                                                                                      |
| 1142  | [assertion {Proof}](#rune-1142)   | authored   | `assertion` in its Proof sense is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the assertion obligation named before work proceeds.   |
| 1143  | [audit log](#rune-1143)           | authored   | `audit log` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the audit log obligation named before work proceeds.                      |
| 1144  | [backtrace](#rune-1144)           | authored   | `backtrace` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the backtrace obligation named before work proceeds.                      |
| 1145  | [baseline {Proof}](#rune-1145)    | authored   | `baseline` in its Proof sense is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the baseline obligation named before work proceeds.     |
| 1146  | [benchmark](#rune-1146)           | authored   | A performance claim forced to meet measurement. Benchmarks are where speed rhetoric either cashes out or dies.                                                                                                                  |
| 1147  | [black-box test](#rune-1147)      | authored   | `black-box test` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the black-box test obligation named before work proceeds.            |
| 1148  | [canary check](#rune-1148)        | authored   | `canary check` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the canary check obligation named before work proceeds.                |
| 1149  | [check](#rune-1149)               | authored   | `check` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the check obligation named before work proceeds.                              |
| 1150  | [code coverage](#rune-1150)       | authored   | `code coverage` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the code coverage obligation named before work proceeds.              |
| 1151  | [conformance](#rune-1151)         | authored   | `conformance` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the conformance obligation named before work proceeds.                  |
| 1152  | [contract test](#rune-1152)       | authored   | A promise checked at the seam between systems. Use it when mocks are too flattering and end-to-end tests are too blunt.                                                                                                         |
| 1153  | [core dump {Proof}](#rune-1153)   | authored   | `core dump` in its Proof sense is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the core dump obligation named before work proceeds.   |
| 1154  | [counter {Proof}](#rune-1154)     | authored   | `counter` in its Proof sense is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the counter obligation named before work proceeds.       |
| 1155  | [dashboard](#rune-1155)           | authored   | `dashboard` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the dashboard obligation named before work proceeds.                      |
| 1156  | [debugger](#rune-1156)            | authored   | `debugger` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the debugger obligation named before work proceeds.                        |
| 1157  | [diagnostic](#rune-1157)          | authored   | `diagnostic` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the diagnostic obligation named before work proceeds.                    |
| 1158  | [error budget](#rune-1158)        | authored   | `error budget` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the error budget obligation named before work proceeds.                |
| 1159  | [evaluation {Proof}](#rune-1159)  | authored   | `evaluation` in its Proof sense is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the evaluation obligation named before work proceeds. |
| 1160  | [expectation](#rune-1160)         | authored   | `expectation` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the expectation obligation named before work proceeds.                  |
| 1161  | [fixture](#rune-1161)             | authored   | `fixture` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the fixture obligation named before work proceeds.                          |
| 1162  | [flame graph](#rune-1162)         | authored   | `flame graph` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the flame graph obligation named before work proceeds.                  |
| 1163  | [fuzzing](#rune-1163)             | authored   | Throw weird inputs at the system until hidden assumptions break.                                                                                                                                                                |
| 1164  | [gauge](#rune-1164)               | authored   | `gauge` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the gauge obligation named before work proceeds.                              |
| 1165  | [golden test](#rune-1165)         | authored   | `golden test` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the golden test obligation named before work proceeds.                  |
| 1166  | [harness](#rune-1166)             | authored   | `harness` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the harness obligation named before work proceeds.                          |
| 1167  | [health probe](#rune-1167)        | authored   | `health probe` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the health probe obligation named before work proceeds.                |
| 1168  | [histogram](#rune-1168)           | authored   | `histogram` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the histogram obligation named before work proceeds.                      |
| 1169  | [hypothesis](#rune-1169)          | authored   | `hypothesis` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the hypothesis obligation named before work proceeds.                    |
| 1170  | [incident report](#rune-1170)     | authored   | `incident report` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the incident report obligation named before work proceeds.          |
| 1171  | [integration test](#rune-1171)    | authored   | Test that multiple real parts cooperate correctly together.                                                                                                                                                                     |
| 1172  | [invariant](#rune-1172)           | authored   | A law that must remain true while the rest of the system is allowed to move. If you cannot say the invariant, you probably do not yet understand the change.                                                                    |
| 1173  | [load test](#rune-1173)           | authored   | `load test` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the load test obligation named before work proceeds.                      |
| 1174  | [log](#rune-1174)                 | authored   | A recorded stream of events or decisions for later reading.                                                                                                                                                                     |
| 1175  | [logger](#rune-1175)              | authored   | `logger` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the logger obligation named before work proceeds.                            |
| 1176  | [metric](#rune-1176)              | authored   | A numeric measure that can be tracked over time.                                                                                                                                                                                |
| 1177  | [mock](#rune-1177)                | authored   | `mock` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the mock obligation named before work proceeds.                                |
| 1178  | [model check](#rune-1178)         | authored   | `model check` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the model check obligation named before work proceeds.                  |
| 1179  | [monitor](#rune-1179)             | authored   | `monitor` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the monitor obligation named before work proceeds.                          |
| 1180  | [observability](#rune-1180)       | authored   | Enough internal evidence exists that you can infer what the system is doing from the outside. Not logs alone: legible state under pressure.                                                                                     |
| 1181  | [oracle](#rune-1181)              | authored   | `oracle` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the oracle obligation named before work proceeds.                            |
| 1182  | [postcondition](#rune-1182)       | authored   | `postcondition` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the postcondition obligation named before work proceeds.              |
| 1183  | [precondition](#rune-1183)        | authored   | `precondition` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the precondition obligation named before work proceeds.                |
| 1184  | [profiler](#rune-1184)            | authored   | A measurement tool that reveals where time or allocation is being spent.                                                                                                                                                        |
| 1185  | [proof](#rune-1185)               | authored   | `proof` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the proof obligation named before work proceeds.                              |
| 1186  | [property-based test](#rune-1186) | authored   | Instead of checking one remembered example, ask whether a law survives many generated cases. Use it when you care more about the invariant than the anecdote.                                                                   |
| 1187  | [regression test](#rune-1187)     | authored   | A test written so yesterday's bug does not return tomorrow.                                                                                                                                                                     |
| 1188  | [replay](#rune-1188)              | authored   | `replay` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the replay obligation named before work proceeds.                            |
| 1189  | [sampling](#rune-1189)            | authored   | `sampling` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the sampling obligation named before work proceeds.                        |
| 1190  | [sanity check](#rune-1190)        | authored   | `sanity check` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the sanity check obligation named before work proceeds.                |
| 1191  | [simulation](#rune-1191)          | authored   | `simulation` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the simulation obligation named before work proceeds.                    |
| 1192  | [smoke test](#rune-1192)          | authored   | A fast confidence check that the basic path still breathes.                                                                                                                                                                     |
| 1193  | [snapshot test](#rune-1193)       | authored   | `snapshot test` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the snapshot test obligation named before work proceeds.              |
| 1194  | [soak test](#rune-1194)           | authored   | `soak test` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the soak test obligation named before work proceeds.                      |
| 1195  | [solver](#rune-1195)              | authored   | `solver` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the solver obligation named before work proceeds.                            |
| 1196  | [span](#rune-1196)                | authored   | `span` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the span obligation named before work proceeds.                                |
| 1197  | [stack trace](#rune-1197)         | authored   | `stack trace` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the stack trace obligation named before work proceeds.                  |
| 1198  | [static checker](#rune-1198)      | authored   | `static checker` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the static checker obligation named before work proceeds.            |
| 1199  | [stress test](#rune-1199)         | authored   | `stress test` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the stress test obligation named before work proceeds.                  |
| 1200  | [stub](#rune-1200)                | authored   | `stub` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the stub obligation named before work proceeds.                                |
| 1201  | [symbolic execution](#rune-1201)  | authored   | `symbolic execution` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the symbolic execution obligation named before work proceeds.    |
| 1202  | [telemetry](#rune-1202)           | authored   | Measurements leaving the system in a form other systems can compare, store, and alert on. Shadow: metric exhaust without explanatory power.                                                                                     |
| 1203  | [theorem {Proof}](#rune-1203)     | authored   | `theorem` in its Proof sense is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the theorem obligation named before work proceeds.       |
| 1204  | [trace {Proof}](#rune-1204)       | authored   | A preserved path through execution. Use it when the question is not only what failed, but where the causal thread first bent.                                                                                                   |
| 1205  | [tracer](#rune-1205)              | authored   | `tracer` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the tracer obligation named before work proceeds.                            |
| 1206  | [triage](#rune-1206)              | authored   | `triage` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the triage obligation named before work proceeds.                            |
| 1207  | [unit test](#rune-1207)           | authored   | Test the smallest meaningful unit in isolation.                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| 1208  | [validation](#rune-1208)          | authored   | `validation` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the validation obligation named before work proceeds.                    |
| 1209  | [verifier](#rune-1209)            | authored   | `verifier` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the verifier obligation named before work proceeds.                        |
| 1210  | [white-box test](#rune-1210)      | authored   | `white-box test` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the white-box test obligation named before work proceeds.            |

## Entries

### [1140] acceptance test {#rune-1140}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `acceptance test` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the acceptance test obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `acceptance test` at rune 1140 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1141] alert {#rune-1141}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A threshold that wakes humans or automation when evidence crosses concern.

**Shadow:** misusing `alert` at rune 1141 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

**Pocket gloss:** A threshold that wakes humans or automation when evidence crosses concern.

### [1142] assertion {Proof} {#rune-1142}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `assertion` in its Proof sense is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the assertion obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `assertion` at rune 1142 in the Proof lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1143] audit log {#rune-1143}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `audit log` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the audit log obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `audit log` at rune 1143 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1144] backtrace {#rune-1144}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `backtrace` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the backtrace obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `backtrace` at rune 1144 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1145] baseline {Proof} {#rune-1145}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `baseline` in its Proof sense is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the baseline obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `baseline` at rune 1145 in the Proof lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1146] benchmark {#rune-1146}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A performance claim forced to meet measurement. Benchmarks are where speed rhetoric either cashes out or dies.

**Shadow:** when a benchmark optimizes the measured path and neglects the production path.

**Pocket gloss:** Force a performance claim to meet measurement.

### [1147] black-box test {#rune-1147}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `black-box test` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the black-box test obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `black-box test` at rune 1147 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1148] canary check {#rune-1148}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `canary check` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the canary check obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `canary check` at rune 1148 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1149] check {#rune-1149}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `check` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the check obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `check` at rune 1149 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1150] code coverage {#rune-1150}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `code coverage` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the code coverage obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `code coverage` at rune 1150 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1151] conformance {#rune-1151}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `conformance` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the conformance obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `conformance` at rune 1151 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1152] contract test {#rune-1152}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A promise checked at the seam between systems. Use it when mocks are too flattering and end-to-end tests are too blunt.

**Shadow:** when the contract test freezes provider quirks instead of public obligations.

**Pocket gloss:** A promise checked at the seam between systems.

### [1153] core dump {Proof} {#rune-1153}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `core dump` in its Proof sense is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the core dump obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `core dump` at rune 1153 in the Proof lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1154] counter {Proof} {#rune-1154}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `counter` in its Proof sense is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the counter obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `counter` at rune 1154 in the Proof lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1155] dashboard {#rune-1155}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `dashboard` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the dashboard obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `dashboard` at rune 1155 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1156] debugger {#rune-1156}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `debugger` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the debugger obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `debugger` at rune 1156 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1157] diagnostic {#rune-1157}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `diagnostic` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the diagnostic obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `diagnostic` at rune 1157 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1158] error budget {#rune-1158}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `error budget` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the error budget obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `error budget` at rune 1158 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1159] evaluation {Proof} {#rune-1159}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `evaluation` in its Proof sense is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the evaluation obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `evaluation` at rune 1159 in the Proof lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1160] expectation {#rune-1160}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `expectation` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the expectation obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `expectation` at rune 1160 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1161] fixture {#rune-1161}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `fixture` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the fixture obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `fixture` at rune 1161 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1162] flame graph {#rune-1162}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `flame graph` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the flame graph obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `flame graph` at rune 1162 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1163] fuzzing {#rune-1163}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Throw weird inputs at the system until hidden assumptions break.

**Shadow:** misusing `fuzzing` at rune 1163 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

**Pocket gloss:** Throw weird inputs at the system until hidden assumptions break.

### [1164] gauge {#rune-1164}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `gauge` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the gauge obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `gauge` at rune 1164 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1165] golden test {#rune-1165}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `golden test` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the golden test obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `golden test` at rune 1165 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1166] harness {#rune-1166}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `harness` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the harness obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `harness` at rune 1166 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1167] health probe {#rune-1167}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `health probe` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the health probe obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `health probe` at rune 1167 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1168] histogram {#rune-1168}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `histogram` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the histogram obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `histogram` at rune 1168 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1169] hypothesis {#rune-1169}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `hypothesis` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the hypothesis obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `hypothesis` at rune 1169 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1170] incident report {#rune-1170}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `incident report` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the incident report obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `incident report` at rune 1170 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1171] integration test {#rune-1171}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Test that multiple real parts cooperate correctly together.

**Shadow:** misusing `integration test` at rune 1171 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

**Pocket gloss:** Test that multiple real parts cooperate correctly together.

### [1172] invariant {#rune-1172}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A law that must remain true while the rest of the system is allowed to move. If you cannot say the invariant, you probably do not yet understand the change.

**Shadow:** when the invariant is asserted in prose but not guarded by checks or tests.

**Pocket gloss:** A law that must remain true while the rest of the system moves.

### [1173] load test {#rune-1173}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `load test` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the load test obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `load test` at rune 1173 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1174] log {#rune-1174}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A recorded stream of events or decisions for later reading.

**Shadow:** misusing `log` at rune 1174 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

**Pocket gloss:** A recorded stream of events or decisions for later reading.

### [1175] logger {#rune-1175}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `logger` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the logger obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `logger` at rune 1175 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1176] metric {#rune-1176}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A numeric measure that can be tracked over time.

**Shadow:** misusing `metric` at rune 1176 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

**Pocket gloss:** A numeric measure that can be tracked over time.

### [1177] mock {#rune-1177}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `mock` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the mock obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `mock` at rune 1177 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1178] model check {#rune-1178}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `model check` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the model check obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `model check` at rune 1178 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1179] monitor {#rune-1179}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `monitor` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the monitor obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `monitor` at rune 1179 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1180] observability {#rune-1180}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Enough internal evidence exists that you can infer what the system is doing from the outside. Not logs alone: legible state under pressure.

**Shadow:** when observable signals exist but no one can connect them to user impact.

**Pocket gloss:** Enough evidence exists to infer what the system is doing from the outside.

### [1181] oracle {#rune-1181}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `oracle` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the oracle obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `oracle` at rune 1181 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1182] postcondition {#rune-1182}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `postcondition` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the postcondition obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `postcondition` at rune 1182 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1183] precondition {#rune-1183}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `precondition` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the precondition obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `precondition` at rune 1183 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1184] profiler {#rune-1184}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A measurement tool that reveals where time or allocation is being spent.

**Shadow:** misusing `profiler` at rune 1184 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

**Pocket gloss:** A measurement tool that reveals where time or allocation is being spent.

### [1185] proof {#rune-1185}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `proof` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the proof obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `proof` at rune 1185 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1186] property-based test {#rune-1186}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Instead of checking one remembered example, ask whether a law survives many generated cases. Use it when you care more about the invariant than the anecdote.

**Shadow:** when generated cases explore input space but miss the property users depend on.

**Pocket gloss:** Test the law across many generated cases instead of one remembered example.

### [1187] regression test {#rune-1187}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A test written so yesterday's bug does not return tomorrow.

**Shadow:** misusing `regression test` at rune 1187 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

**Pocket gloss:** A test written so yesterday's bug does not return tomorrow.

### [1188] replay {#rune-1188}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `replay` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the replay obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `replay` at rune 1188 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1189] sampling {#rune-1189}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `sampling` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the sampling obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `sampling` at rune 1189 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1190] sanity check {#rune-1190}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `sanity check` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the sanity check obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `sanity check` at rune 1190 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1191] simulation {#rune-1191}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `simulation` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the simulation obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `simulation` at rune 1191 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1192] smoke test {#rune-1192}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A fast confidence check that the basic path still breathes.

**Shadow:** misusing `smoke test` at rune 1192 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

**Pocket gloss:** A fast confidence check that the basic path still breathes.

### [1193] snapshot test {#rune-1193}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `snapshot test` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the snapshot test obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `snapshot test` at rune 1193 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1194] soak test {#rune-1194}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `soak test` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the soak test obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `soak test` at rune 1194 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1195] solver {#rune-1195}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `solver` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the solver obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `solver` at rune 1195 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1196] span {#rune-1196}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `span` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the span obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `span` at rune 1196 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1197] stack trace {#rune-1197}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `stack trace` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the stack trace obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `stack trace` at rune 1197 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1198] static checker {#rune-1198}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `static checker` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the static checker obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `static checker` at rune 1198 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1199] stress test {#rune-1199}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `stress test` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the stress test obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `stress test` at rune 1199 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1200] stub {#rune-1200}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `stub` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the stub obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `stub` at rune 1200 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1201] symbolic execution {#rune-1201}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `symbolic execution` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the symbolic execution obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `symbolic execution` at rune 1201 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1202] telemetry {#rune-1202}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Measurements leaving the system in a form other systems can compare, store, and alert on.

**Shadow:** metric exhaust without explanatory power.

**Pocket gloss:** Measurements emitted in a form other systems can store and alert on.

### [1203] theorem {Proof} {#rune-1203}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `theorem` in its Proof sense is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the theorem obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `theorem` at rune 1203 in the Proof lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1204] trace {Proof} {#rune-1204}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A preserved path through execution. Use it when the question is not only what failed, but where the causal thread first bent.

**Shadow:** when trace detail becomes noise and the causal path still remains unclear.

**Pocket gloss:** A preserved path through execution.

### [1205] tracer {#rune-1205}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `tracer` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the tracer obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `tracer` at rune 1205 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1206] triage {#rune-1206}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `triage` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the triage obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `triage` at rune 1206 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1207] unit test {#rune-1207}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Test the smallest meaningful unit in isolation.

**Shadow:** misusing `unit test` at rune 1207 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

**Pocket gloss:** Test the smallest meaningful unit in isolation.

### [1208] validation {#rune-1208}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `validation` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the validation obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `validation` at rune 1208 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1209] verifier {#rune-1209}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `verifier` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the verifier obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `verifier` at rune 1209 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.

### [1210] white-box test {#rune-1210}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** `white-box test` is a testing and observability rune for turn claims into checks, signals, traces, and reviewable evidence; use it when the artifact needs the white-box test obligation named before work proceeds.

**Shadow:** misusing `white-box test` at rune 1210 in the verification lane can let passing checks can miss the property users depend on; verify by requiring the caster to state the invariant, signal, threshold, and decision it supports.
